title: Birth
description: I was born on September 6, 2005, in Shenzhen, China.

title: Hometown
description: Moved back to Changsha, China, my family's hometown. Major accomplishments duriung this time include learning how to walk and talk.

title: Kindergarten
description: Attended kindergarten in Changsha, at a school that no longer exists and is now rebuilt into a retirement home.

title: Elementary School
description: Was often bullied but still made a lot of good connections during this time. Acquired a variety of new skills including: taekwondo, calligraphy, piano, swimming, etc.

title: Immigration
description: Immigrated to Toronto, Canada with my family. Resided in North York, where I completed my elementary school education. During this period, I spent a lot of time learning English and trying to adapt.

title: Middle School
description: I moved to Richmond Hill for Middle School, there, I met some people that are still friends with me till this day. I also won a lot of math competitions (despite being terrible back in China) and learned to play tennis.

title: Early High School
description: At first, I went to an IB high school, but the academic pressure was too strong for me, and the environment was slightly dangerous, so I transferred back to my old school (which offered grades 1 to 12).

title: Late High School
description: It was around grade 11 when I started locking in. Not only did my grades improve significantly, I also involved myself in many extracurricular activities, including co-founding the school's coding club, joining the tennis team, and even starting my own nonprofit intiative aimed at providing support for youth newcomers in Ontario.

title: University
description: I was accepted into the Computer Science program at the University of Toronto. My focus as I first headed into uni was to become more independent, so I got a driver's license and a few part-time jobs during the summers. I also made some good friends here while I studied 10 hours a day, and collborated with them on many projects. Later, I was able to secure 3 internships in both ML and software, which I am very grateful for.
